title: rubus × calvispinus
intro: Rubus × calvispinus, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid of Rubus idaeus and Rubus occidentalis, known for its edible fruits and potential medicinal properties. The plant contains anthocyanins, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its medicinal value. Black raspberry has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving cardiovascular health, and providing anti-aging properties. Herbal preparations of black raspberry, such as teas, juices, and capsules, can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
